sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 10.44 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.10)

statement.latex: Prove that every standard homomorphism$$of$E_n(R) GL_n(R)=GL(V)$into$E_m(S) GL_m(S)=GL(W)$originates from a collineation and a correlation, that is, has the form$x=(g^{-1}xg)f + (h^{-1}xh)(1 - f),$where$g$is a semilinear isomorphism$V_R W_S$(collineation) and$h$is a semilinear isomorphism$V_R _S\! W'_{S^0}$(correlation).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
